input_row.title: Ensure public network access on Recovery Services vaults is Disabled
input_row.description: Disable public network access on Recovery Services vaults to prevent exposure to the internet and reduce the risk of unauthorized access. Use private endpoints and Azure Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) to securely manage access within trusted networks.
input_row.rationale: Disabling public network access improves security by ensuring that a Recovery Services vault is not exposed on the public internet. Impact: Disabling public network access on Recovery Services vaults restricts access to the vault. This enhances security but may require the configuration of private endpoints for any services or users needing access within trusted networks.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Recovery Services vaults. 2. Click the name of a vault. 3. Under Settings, click Networking. 4. Under Public access, ensure that Public network access is set to Deny. 5. Repeat steps 1-4 for each Recovery Services vault. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list Recovery Services vaults: az backup vault list For each Recovery Services vault, run the following command: az backup vault show --resource-group <resource-group> --name <recovery- services-vault> Ensure that under properties, publicNetworkAccess is set to Disabled.  Audit from PowerShell Run the following command to list Recovery Services vaults: Get-AzRecoveryServicesVault Run the following command to get the vault in a resource group with a given name: $vault = Get-AzRecoveryServicesVault -ResourceGroupName <resource-group> - Name <recovery-services-vault> Run the following command to get the public network access setting for the vault: $vault.Properties.PublicNetworkAccess Ensure that the command returns Disabled. Repeat for each Recovery Services vault.
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Recovery Services vaults. 2. Click the name of a vault. 3. Under Settings, click Networking. 4. Under Public access, click the radio button next to Deny. 5. Click Apply. 6. Repeat steps 1-5 for each Recovery Services vault requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each Recovery Services vault requiring remediation, run the following command to disable public network access: az backup vault update --resource-group <resource-group> --name <recovery- services-vault> --public-network-access Disable Remediate from PowerShell For each Recovery Services vault requiring remediation, run the following command to disable public network access: Update-AzRecoveryServicesVault -ResourceGroupName <resource-group> -Name <recovery-services-vault> -PublicNetworkAccess "Disabled" Default Value: Public network access is enabled by default on Recovery Services vaults. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/private-endpoints#deny-public- network-access-to-the-vault 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/backup/vault?view=azure-cli-latest#az- backup-vault-list 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/backup/vault?view=azure-cli-latest#az- backup-vault-show 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/backup/vault?view=azure-cli-latest#az- backup-vault-update 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.recoveryservices/get- azrecoveryservicesvault?view=azps-12.4.0 6. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.recoveryservices/update- azrecoveryservicesvault?view=azps-12.4.0 Additional Information: Private endpoints for Backup can be only created for Recovery Services vaults that don't have any items protected to it. Azure recommends creating a new vault, and outlines a recommended workflow for disabling public network access and creating private endpoints in the following guides: • Create and use private endpoints (v1 experience) for Azure Backup • Create and use private endpoints (v2 experience) for Azure Backup
input_row.impact: Disabling public network access on Recovery Services vaults restricts access to the vault. This enhances security but may require the configuration of private endpoints for any services or users needing access within trusted networks.

Azure recommends creating a new vault, and outlines a recommended workflow for disabling public network access and creating private endpoints in the following guides: • Create and use private endpoints (v1 experience) for Azure Backup • Create and use private endpoints (v2 experience) for Azure Backup

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control is straightforward to audit as it provides clear commands for Azure CLI and PowerShell, which can be executed programmatically. The public network access property for Recovery Services vaults is a standard Azure resource property that can be verified through Azure APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_backup_recovery_services_vault_public_network_access_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would check the configuration of Recovery Services vaults' public network access settings.

- **Commands**: 
  - Use the Azure CLI command: `az backup vault list` to enumerate all Recovery Services vaults.
  - For each vault, run: `az backup vault show --resource-group <resource-group> --name <recovery-services-vault>` to check the properties.

- **Resources to enumerate**: 
  - Iterate over all subscriptions, resource groups, and their respective Recovery Services vaults.

- **Configuration properties/settings to validate**: 
  - Ensure that the `publicNetworkAccess` property in the vault's configuration is set to `Disabled`.

- **Pass/Fail Criteria**: 
  - **Pass**: If all Recovery Services vaults have `publicNetworkAccess` set to `Disabled`.
  - **Fail**: If any Recovery Services vault has `publicNetworkAccess` set to `Enabled`.

- **Example API Endpoint or CLI Command**: 
  - CLI command: `az backup vault show --resource-group <resource-group> --name <recovery-services-vault>` with the expectation that it returns `{ "properties": { "publicNetworkAccess": "Disabled" } }`.

This structure allows for a clear and efficient compliance check, ensuring that all Recovery Services vaults are secured against unauthorized public access.
